metadata.title: Why There Is Uncertainty: Heisenberg's Relation as an Exact Finite Count
metadata.description:
Smithian Fold Theory derives uncertainty as conservation of a finite support count across two conjugate descriptions of one state. At depth k, the state contains N=2^k cells and the minimum position-frequency support product is exactly N. The machine certificates verify 2 x 4 = 8 at depth three, 2 x 16 = 32 at depth five, and full conjugate support for a localized state.

An independent variance route gives the same object: at depth two the minimal variance product is exactly 1/16, equal to the independently generated floor 1/2^(2k). Equality, a wider-state control above the floor and identity with spacing squared are all machine-checked.

The result replaces unexplained continuum fuzziness with an exact, transparent construction from one self-proven theorem, zero axioms and zero fitted parameters. Derivation, execution and measurement remain connected throughout.

Scientific author and publication authority: Maria Smith, Ernos Labs. Open source: Smithian Fold Theory of Everything.
metadata.notes: Authoritative rebuilt edition. The finished PDF is the primary preview; the Markdown source follows.
